titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.25

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=638 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:28:28.779725+00 | translation moa=639 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:28:30.357351+00

div.p:
Willelmus de Morevill’ constabularius Scot’ Omnibus amicis et probis hominibus suis presentibus et futuris Salutem .
Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse et hac carta mea confirmasse Iacobo de Laudun terram de laudin per
istas diuisas . Scilicet de polba  usque ad diuisas de Strathouan . et de aqua que uocatur Yrewin usque ad
diuisas de heglesham . Tenendum illi et heredibus suis extra forestam et quietam de foresta de me et
heredibus meis in feudo et hereditate . In bosco et plano . in pratis et pascuis . in viis et semitis . in monaste
rio et Molendinis . et in Omnibus aliis ad predictam terram iuste pertinentibus . libere . quiete . plene . integre .
et honorifice  per seruicium vnius militis . Hiis Testibus . Christiana sponsa mea . Godfrido de Ros . Roberto
filio Werbald’ . Steffano filio Ric’ . Willelmo Mansell’ . alano de thirlestan . petro de la hage . henrico de
Saincler . Iuone de veteri ponte . duncano filio Comitis dunc’ . Ric’ filio Gileb’ . arthuro de ardrossan .
Roberto de logan . Reginaldo de Ros . Iacobo fratre eius . Ric’ de Morevill’ . Ric’ de Nehii . helia filio hugonis .
alano de Saincler Toma Anglico Willelmo filio Iohannis . herberto . et Willelmo fratre eius . Ric’ de Witton’ . Iohanne clerico .

p.cl: William de Morville, constable of Scotland, to all his own friends and worthy men, present and future, greeting.

me to have given, conceded, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to James of Loudon the land of Loudon, by these boundaries, that is to say, from Polbaith to the boundaries of Strathaven, and from the water which is called Irvine as far as the boundaries of Eaglesham. For him and his heirs to hold outside the forest, and quit of forest, of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in roads and paths, in monastery and mills, and in all other things rightfully pertaining to the aforesaid land, freely, quitly, fully, wholly, and honourably by the service of one knight.

With these witnesses: Christina my spouse; Godfrey de Ros; Robert son of Warnebald; Stephen son of Richard; William Mansell; Alan of Thirlestane; Peter de la Haig; Henry de Sinclair; Ivo de Vieuxpont; Duncan son of Earl Duncan; Richard fitz Gilbert; Arthur of Ardrossan; Robert of Logan; Reginald de Ros; James his brother; Richard de Morville; Richard de Néhou; Elias son of Hugh; Alan de Sinclair; Thomas English; William son of John; Herbert, and William his brother; Richard of Whitton; John the clerk.
